name: Kibbee Bissaneeyeh - Lamb W/wheat, Baked W/Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
- 1.5 cups Fine bulgur
- 2 pounds Ground lamb; finely ground
- 1/4 cup Cold water
- 2 Medium onions; grated
- 2 teaspoons Salt
- 1/2 teaspoon Ground allspice
- 1 pinch Nutmeg; grated
- 1 pinch Cayenne pepper (optional)
- 3 tablespoons Butter; room temperature
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Make the stuffing: Heat the oil in a saucepan and add the coarsely ground lamb, pine nuts, onion, salt, and allspice. Cook, stirring until browned. Set aside to cool as you make the kibbee base.
3. Make the kibbee base: Soak the bulgur in water to cover for 10 minutes or until softened. Drain well. Add the finely ground lamb, cold water, and onions and knead the mixture well. Keep moistening your hands so the mixture does not stick to them. Knead in the spices.
4. Form a kibbee of about 1 1/2 inches in height. About 10 inches in diameter is fine for 1 1/2-inch high kibbee.
5. Butter a round or rectangular baking pan with about 1 tablespoon of the butter.
6. With wet hands, press half the kibbee mixture into the pan, patting it level. Spread the stuffing evenly over the base.
7. Cover with the rest of the kibbee mixture and pat with moistened hands.
8. With a knife dipped in cold water, cut a diamond pattern through the layers.
9. Dot with the remaining butter.
10. Bake for 10 minutes at 375°F, then lower the heat to 325°F. Bake for another 10 to 15 minutes, or until browned and bubbling. Do not overcook.
11. Serve hot with yogurt and salad.
Instead of making one big kibbee, you can make "meatballs." Take about 2 tablespoons of meat mixture and flatten in the palm of your hand. Place about 1-2 teaspoons of filling in the middle. Then take another 2 tablespoons of meat mixture, flatten it, and place over the meat with the filling. Shape into oval-shaped meatballs. Bake the same way as above.
